title: Taco Bell nutrition table (part 2) — Mexican Mocha, Pink Lemonade Frost, Vanilla Crema, Wild Strawberry
html:
TL;DR

The middle of the three Taco Bell beverage scrapes (4:56:23 a.m., 10 seconds after part 1). This page covers the rest of the chillers + agua refrescas: Mexican Chocolate Churro Chiller 16/20oz (430/570), Mexican Mocha Coffee Hot/Iced/Latte Chiller variants (160–550), Pineapple Lime Rockstar Refresca (50/50), Pink Lemonade Frost 16/20oz (230/320), Strawberry Matcha Chiller 16/20oz (510/680), Strawberry Passionfruit family, Tropical Punch Rockstar Refresca, Vanilla Crema Coffee Hot/Iced/Latte Chiller variants, and Wild Strawberry Churro Chiller (440/580).

Why it's in the graph

The companion to 2025 07 18 screenshot 2025 07 18 at 4 56 13 am e3d1a9e8 and 2025 07 18 screenshot 2025 07 18 at 4 56 30 am db1a0de7 — same 4:56 a.m. data-grab session for the Brotein / BroMenu backend. Almost zero protein on this entire page (most rows show 1–6g), which is the obvious anabolic scoring story: drinks at Taco Bell are calorie traps with ~zero anabolic value. The Strawberry Matcha Chiller 20oz (680 cal, ~14g sat fat, ~62g sugar) is essentially a milkshake.

What's actually on the page

  • Mexican Chocolate Churro Chiller 16oz 430 / 20oz 570 (15g sat fat at 20oz).
  • Mexican Mocha Coffee Hot 160, Iced 16/20oz 260/280, Latte Chiller 16/20oz 430/550.
  • Pineapple Lime Rockstar Refresca S/L 50/50 — zero-sugar option.
  • Pink Lemonade Frost 230/320 cal.
  • Strawberry Matcha Chiller 510/680 — heaviest drink on the page.
  • Strawberry Passionfruit Agua Refresca / Limonada 80/100/220 cal range.
  • Tropical Punch Rockstar Refresca ~similar to Pineapple Lime.
  • Vanilla Crema Coffee Hot 220, Iced 16/20oz 220/220, Latte Chiller 390/510.
  • Wild Strawberry Churro Chiller 16/20oz 440/580.
  • Macro columns visible but heavily OCR-mangled — protein column obvious from spacing alone.

Loose ends

  • The "(more info)" anchor suggests Yahir was on the Taco Bell website menu page (where each item links to a detail panel), not the corporate PDF — different scrape strategy implications for BroMenu.
  • Tacos & burritos finish on part 3.
